question.text: An option on crude oil has a premium of Rs 150 and intrinsic value of Rs 0. This option is:

answer.text: Either ATM or OTM — its entire premium consists of time value
ITM because the premium is positive
Deep ITM with no time value
Cannot be determined without more information

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Zero intrinsic value means the option is either ATM (market = strike) or OTM (unfavorable market vs strike). The Rs 150 premium is entirely time value. **The Trap:** Positive premium doesn't mean ITM — even OTM options have time value (positive premium) before expiry. Deep ITM options usually have minimal time value. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers premium decomposition. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Buying options with 100% time value means the entire premium erodes by expiry unless the option moves ITM — these are speculative bets on direction and timing.
